variant_label: Domestic cigarette consumption (Ministry of Health estimate)
notes: Three conflicting official/unofficial estimates given in the same article -- see the two rows below. Included together, not averaged, to preserve the actual reported range. [source: Tobacco Asia, 'Iran in Focus', 16 July 2018]

variant_label: Domestic cigarette consumption (Ministry of Industries Mining and Trade estimate)
notes: Alternative official estimate, same year -- illustrates the poor state of post-privatization consumption data. [source: Tobacco Asia, 'Iran in Focus', 16 July 2018]
Unofficial estimate, described in the source as exceeding both official figures. [source: Tobacco Asia, 'Iran in Focus', 16 July 2018]
Equivalent to 12.5% of Iran's then population of ~80 million; only ~4% of smokers were female. [source: Tobacco Asia, 'Iran in Focus', 16 July 2018]

variant_label: Share of tobacco leaf that must be imported
notes: Reflects the collapse of domestic cultivation capacity since the Pahlavi-era self-sufficiency levels (compare 1940: 13,500 tons annual output, self-sufficient -- see sibling dataset iranica-tobacco-monopoly-narrative-series-1890-1995). [source: Tobacco Asia, 'Iran in Focus', 16 July 2018]

Given as a range 'hovering around 15-20%'; midpoint used. ITC (successor to the historic state Dokhaniyat monopoly) was privatized in 2012; by 2018 the market leader was Japan Tobacco International (JTI) with just over 50% share -- a striking reversal from a century of state monopoly. [source: Tobacco Asia, 'Iran in Focus', 16 July 2018]
